title: `buildah build` on GitHub Actions lets `tar` on Alpine 3.20 fail with `Operation not permitted` errors
body:
**Description**

When running Buildah 1.23.1 on GitHub Actions (ubuntu-22.04 v20240616.1.0; [view software list](https://github.com/actions/runner-images/blob/ubuntu22/20240616.1/images/ubuntu/Ubuntu2204-Readme.md)) to build an Alpine 3.20 image from a Dockerfile (using `buildah build`), `tar` fails with `Cannot change mode to ***: Operation not permitted` errors.

This is a weirdly specific and nasty issue. I can't reproduce it locally, only with GitHub Actions. I can't reproduce it with Alpine 3.19. I can't reproduce it with any other software than `tar`. I can't reproduce it with Docker. The only reason I report it here is the last: Since it works with Docker, it must at least be somehow related to Buildah, Podman, et. al. However, it doesn't seem to be related to the `tar` archive itself: in my reproducer below I'm using PHP's source archive just because I encountered the issue with PHP first, the issue persists with other archives too, e.g. Apache's source archive.

I've created a stripped down reproducer repo below that contains just a `Dockerfile` and a GitHub workflow. The GitHub workflow basically just calls `buildah unshare buildah build .` to build a container image from said Dockerfile. Besides installing `tar`, `curl`, and `xz`, said Dockerfile just downloads the `tar` archive and attempts to extract it. `tar` fails with `Cannot change mode to ***: Operation not permitted` errors.

/kind bug

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a Git repository with the following files, create a GitHub repository and push the files:
    - `Dockerfile`
      ```Dockerfile
      FROM alpine:3.20

      RUN set -eux; \
          apk add --no-cache tar curl xz

      RUN set -eux; \
          mkdir -p /usr/src; \
          curl -fsSL -o /usr/src/php.tar.xz "https://www.php.net/distributions/php-8.3.8.tar.xz"; \
          \
          mkdir -p /usr/src/php; \
          tar -Jxf /usr/src/php.tar.xz -C /usr/src/php --strip-components=1
      ```
    - `.github/workflows/test.yml`
      ```yaml
      name: Test

      on:
        push:
          branches: [ main ]

      jobs:
        build:
          runs-on: ubuntu-latest

          steps:
            - name: System info
              shell: bash -x {0}
              run: |
                apt list buildah
                buildah version
                podman version
                cat /etc/os-release
                uname -a

            - name: Checkout
              uses: actions/checkout@v3

            - name: Build
              run: |
                buildah unshare buildah build .
      ```
3. Await the GitHub workflow to run and check the job's log.

**Describe the results you received:**

The workflow fails, specifically `tar` bails with `Cannot change mode to ***: Operation not permitted` errors:

<details>
<summary>View relevant workflow step logs</summary>

```
﻿2024-06-21T14:09:37.1402497Z ##[group]Run buildah unshare buildah build .

[… snip …]

2024-06-21T14:09:42.0039872Z time="2024-06-21T14:09:42Z" level=error msg="exit status 2"

2024-06-21T14:09:42.0085866Z ##[error]Process completed with exit code 2.
```

</details>

Download full logs: [`logs_25170431238.zip`](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15929130/logs_25170431238.zip)

**Describe the results you expected:**

The GitHub workflow should run just fine.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/jammy,now 1.23.1+ds1-2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.23.1
Go Version:      go1.17
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.16.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"

VERSION="22.04.4 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)"

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux fv-az1016-883 6.5.0-1022-azure #23~22.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Thu May  9 17:59:24 UTC 2024 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

*File doesn't exist* (i.e. system defaults)

comments.body:
I'm going to immediately close this, since I found that the issue has been fixed in later Buildah, Podman, et. al. versions. I'm publishing the issue here nevertheless, since I was finished writing this issue anyway and it might help someone else with the same problem (searching for the `tar` error yields very few results on Google, so it might actually be found…).

The solution is to run the GitHub workflow on a `ubuntu-24.04` image (with Buildah 1.33.7, Podman 4.9.3, etc.). Note that GitHub considers these runner images to still be in beta as of today.
